page: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server instance featuring three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs based on the Turing architecture, providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM. The system is powered by an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It includes 84.0 GB of system memory, equivalent to 5.25 GB per core, and is provisioned with 400 GB of local SSD storage under a KVM hypervisor. Network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong capabilities in specific workloads, particularly LLM inference. The server achieves top-tier results for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models. General compute performance is moderate, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU benchmarks falling into the average category. While the Passmark memory score is strong, memory bandwidth tests reveal a tradeoff: read-only small-block cached bandwidth is average at 990.44 GB/sec, but large-block uncached write bandwidth is weak, registering at 25.38 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers a high density of GPU resources relative to its 16-core CPU allocation, making it a viable option for parallelized graphics rendering, ray tracing, and machine learning inference without the overhead of a larger CPU footprint. The inclusion of 400 GB of local SSD storage and a complimentary public IPv4 address adds to its utility. Appropriate workloads include LLM inference, ray tracing, image processing, and parallelized GPU-bound computations.
description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server configured with three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs, delivering a total of 48 GB of VRAM. It features an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz, 84.0 GB of system memory, and 400 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under a KVM hypervisor, the instance provides 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark data highlights top-tier performance in LLM inference tasks across various model sizes, alongside average general CPU execution. However, a notable tradeoff is its weak large-block memory write bandwidth of 25.38 GB/sec. This hardware profile is optimized for GPU-heavy workloads, including machine learning inference, ray tracing, and image processing, offering high GPU density relative to its compute resources.
og_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs with 48 GB total VRAM, 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, and 400 GB of local SSD storage.
meta_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a KVM-based GPU server with three NVIDIA RTX 5000 GPUs, 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, and 400 GB SSD storage.
tagline: A triple-GPU server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores and 48 GB of total VRAM, optimized for parallelized graphics and language model inference.
bullet_points: Three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM
16 dedicated Intel Xeon W-3235 physical cores running at 3.3 GHz
Top-tier benchmark performance for LLM inference and prompt processing
400 GB of local SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Weak large-block memory write bandwidth of 25.38 GB/sec as a hardware tradeoff
